devanagari: कुरुक्षेत्रे सूर्योपरागपर्वणि यदुभिः सह कुरूणां नन्दादिगोपानां च समागमः । श्रीशुक उवाच । अथैकदा द्वारवत्यां वसतो रामकृष्णयोः । सूर्योपर...
english_devnagari: kurukṣetre sūryoparāgaparvaṇi yadubhiḥ saha kurūṇāṃ nandādigopānāṃ ca samāgamaḥ śrīśuka uvāca athaikadā dvāravatyāṃ vasato rāmakṛṣṇayoḥ sūryoparāgaḥ sumahānāsītkalpakṣaye yathā

translation: With loud chantings of Vedic hymns and playing of instrumental music, and in accompaniment of the flourish of the sounds of conchs and beating of drums, they went on singing and repeating the name of Kṛṣṇa and Govinda continuously. In this way, they filled all the quarters with great noise as they went, O best of Kurus.

devanagari: तं ज्ञात्वा मनुजा राजन्पुरस्तादेव सर्वतः । समन्तपञ्चकं क्षेत्रं ययुः श्रेयोविधित्सया ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ jñātvā manujā rājanpurastādeva sarvataḥ samantapañcakaṃ kṣetraṃ yayuḥ śreyovidhitsayā

translation: With loud chantings of Vedic hymns and playing of instrumental music, and in accompaniment of the flourish of the sounds of conchs and beating of drums, they went on singing and repeating the name of Kṛṣṇa and Govinda continuously. In this way, they filled all the quarters with great noise as they went, O best of Kurus.

devanagari: निःक्षत्रियां महीं कुर्वन्रामः शस्त्रभृतां वरः । नृपाणां रुधिरौघेण यत्र चक्रे महाह्रदान् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: niḥkṣatriyāṃ mahīṃ kurvanrāmaḥ śastrabhṛtāṃ varaḥ nṛpāṇāṃ rudhiraugheṇa yatra cakre mahāhradān

translation: As they reached the holy place Syamantapañcaka, well known all over the three worlds, they built up hutments (dwelling places) with thatched roof, matting and wood (lit. with long grass and sticks).

devanagari: ईजे च भगवान्रामो यत्रास्पृष्टोऽपि कर्मणा । लोकं सङ्ग्राहयन्नीशो यथान्योऽघापनुत्तये ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: īje ca bhagavānrāmo yatrāspṛṣṭo lokaṃ saṅgrāhayannīśo yathānyo

translation: Tents with blankets, deerskin and canvass were pitched up for kings. They were hundreds and thousands in number and had a variety of forms.

devanagari: महत्यां तीर्थयात्रायां तत्रागन्भारतीः प्रजाः । वृष्णयश्च तथाक्रूर वसुदेवाहुकादयः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: mahatyāṃ tīrthayātrāyāṃ tatrāganbhāratīḥ prajāḥ vṛṣṇayaśca tathākrūra vasudevāhukādayaḥ

translation: There arrived a number of kings, thousands of Brāhmaṇas, Vaiśyas and Śūdras and innumerable persons of lower castes.

devanagari: ययुर्भारत तत्क्षेत्रं स्वमघं क्षपयिष्णवः । गदप्रद्युम्नसाम्बाद्याः सुचन्द्रशुकसारणैः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: yayurbhārata tatkṣetraṃ svamaghaṃ kṣapayiṣṇavaḥ gadapradyumnasāmbādyāḥ sucandraśukasāraṇaiḥ
translation: And noble-souled sages of high reputation and of praiseworthy observance of vows, with preceptors, some maintainers of consecrated fire (agni-hotrins), some life-long celibate students and householders.

devanagari: आस्तेऽनिरुद्धो रक्षायां कृतवर्मा च यूथपः । ते रथैर्देवधिष्ण्याभैः हयैश्च तरलप्लवैः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: āste te rathairdevadhiṣṇyābhaiḥ hayaiśca taralaplavaiḥ
translation: The blind, the deaf, the hunchbacks, dwarfs and physically handicapped people, persons with retarded intelligence, dumb persons—all are desirous of being absolved of their past actions.

devanagari: गजैर्नदद्भिरभ्राभैः नृभिर्विद्याधरद्युभिः । व्यरोचन्त महातेजाः पथि काञ्चनमालिनः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: gajairnadadbhirabhrābhaiḥ nṛbhirvidyādharadyubhiḥ vyarocanta mahātejāḥ pathi kāñcanamālinaḥ
translation: All of them adorned their heads with the dust on the lotus-feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa, beautified with ornaments like bracelets, armlets (both on the lower and upper part of the forearm), garlands and ear-rings.

devanagari: दिव्यस्रग्वस्त्रसन्नाहाः कलत्रैः खेचरा इव । तत्र स्नात्वा महाभागा उपोष्य सुसमाहिताः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: divyasragvastrasannāhāḥ kalatraiḥ khecarā iva tatra snātvā mahābhāgā upoṣya susamāhitāḥ
translation: Like celestials ranging through the sky, along with their wives, they put on heavenly garlands, rich clothes. Arriving at Syamantapañcaka, those highly blessed people took their bath, observed a fast and were perfectly serene and self possessed in mind.

devanagari: ब्राह्मणेभ्यो ददुर्धेनूः वासःस्रग्रुक्ममालिनीः । रामह्रदेषु विधिवत् पुनराप्लुत्य वृष्णयः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: brāhmaṇebhyo dadurdhenūḥ vāsaḥsragrukmamālinīḥ rāmahradeṣu vidhivat punarāplutya vṛṣṇayaḥ
translation: They gave to Brāhmaṇas gifts of cows covering them with clothes and adoring them with garlands and chains of gold. (And after the eclipse was over) the Vṛṣṇis again took a bath as per formalities in the pools created by Paraśarāma.

devanagari: ददुः स्वन्नं द्विजाग्र्येभ्यः कृष्णे नो भक्तिरस्त्विति । स्वयं च तदनुज्ञाता वृष्णयः कृष्णदेवताः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: daduḥ svannaṃ dvijāgryebhyaḥ kṛṣṇe no bhaktirastviti svayaṃ ca tadanujñātā vṛṣṇayaḥ kṛṣṇadevatāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ jñā class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ jñā class 3 verb], [nominative plural from √ jñā class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ jñā class 9 verb]

[nominative plural from √ jñā class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ jñā class 3 verb], [accusative plural from √ jñā class 3 verb], [nominative plural from √ jñā class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ jñā class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ jñā class 9 verb]

translation: The Vṛṣṇis to whom Kṛṣṇa was their deity, served delicious food to prominent Brāhmaṇas and prayed, “May we be blest with constant devotion to Kṛṣṇa. With the permission of the Brāhmaṇas, they broke their fast. And they took rest according to their wish and pleasure under the dense, cool shade of trees. They interviewed their friendly kings and relatives who congregated there (for the same religious purpose).

devanagari: भुक्त्वोपविविशुः कामं स्निग्धच्छायाङ्घ्रिपाङ्घ्रिषु । तत्रागतांस्ते ददृशुः सुहृत्संबन्धिनो नृपान् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: bhuktvopaviviśuḥ kāmaṃ snigdhacchāyāṅghripāṅghriṣu tatrāgatāṃste dadṛśuḥ suhṛtsaṃbandhino nṛpān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ snih class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ snih class 4 verb]
[vocative single from √ snih class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ snih class 4 verb]

translation: They saw kings (their friends and relatives) of Matsya, Uśīnara, Kosala, Vidarbha, Kuru, Sṛñjaya, Kamboja, Kekaya, Madra, Kuntī, Ānarta and Kerala and hundreds of many other kings who were their allies or strangers. They met their friends and well-wishers like Nanda and other cowherds and Gopīs (cowherd women) who were anxiously longing to meet them.

devanagari: मत्स्योशीनरकौशल्य विदर्भकुरुसृञ्जयान् । काम्बोजकैकयान् मद्रान् कुन्तीनानर्तकेरलान् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: matsyośīnarakauśalya vidarbhakurusṛñjayān kāmbojakaikayān madrān kuntīnānartakeralān

devanagari: अन्यांश्चैवात्मपक्षीयान् परांश्च शतशो नृप । नन्दादीन् सुहृदो गोपान् गोपीश्चोत्कण्ठिताश्चिरम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: anyāṃścaivātmapakṣīyān parāṃśca śataśo nṛpa nandādīn suhṛdo gopān gopīścotkaṇṭhitāściram
translation: They saw kings (their friends and relatives) of Matsya, Uśīnara, Kosala, Vidarbha, Kuru, Sṛñjaya, Kamboja, Kekaya, Madra, Kuntī, Ānarta and Kerala and hundreds of many other kings who were their allies or strangers. They met their friends and well-wishers like Nanda and other cowherds and Gopīs (cowherd women) who were anxiously longing to meet them.

devanagari: अन्योन्यसन्दर्शनहर्षरंहसा । प्रोत्फुल्लहृद्वक्त्रसरोरुहश्रियः । आश्लिष्य गाढं नयनैः स्रवज्जला । हृष्यत्त्वचो रुद्धगिरो ययुर्मुदम् ॥ १५...
english_devnagari: anyonyasandarśanaharṣaraṃhasā protphullahṛdvaktrasaroruhaśriyaḥ āśliṣya gāḍhaṃ nayanaiḥ sravajjalā hṛṣyattvaco ruddhagiro yayurmudam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb]

translation: Owing to overwhelming ecstatic joy of mutually looking at each other, their lotus-like hearts and faces became fully blossomed and full of splendour. They hugged each other in close embrace, with tears of joy trickling from their eyes; their hair stood on their ends; their voice choked with emotion and faltering—all of them were transported with joy.

devanagari: स्त्रियश्च संवीक्ष्य मिथोऽतिसौहृद । स्मितामलापाङ्गदृशोऽभिरेभिरे । स्तनैः स्तनान् कुङ्कुमपङ्करूषितान् । निहत्य दोर्भिः प्रणयाश्रुलोचनाः...
english_devnagari: striyaśca saṃvīkṣya mitho smitāmalāpāṅgadṛśo stanaiḥ stanān kuṅkumapaṅkarūṣitān nihatya dorbhiḥ praṇayāśrulocanāḥ
translation: Looking at each other with affectionate side-glances and exceedingly friendly smiles, the women also embraced each other closely, pressing against each other their breasts which were besmeared with saffron and their eyes full of tears of joy and affection.

devanagari: ततोऽभिवाद्य ते वृद्धान् यविष्ठैरभिवादिताः । स्वागतं कुशलं पृष्ट्वा चक्रुः कृष्णकथा मिथः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tato svāgataṃ kuśalaṃ pṛṣṭvā cakruḥ kṛṣṇakathā mithaḥ
translation: After paying respects to the elders, they were bowed by their juniors. Welcoming each other, they enquired after the well-being (of their friends and relatives) and they told each other the stories of Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: पृथा भ्रातॄन् स्वसॄर्वीक्ष्य तत्पुत्रान् पितरावपि । भ्रातृपत्‍नीर्मुकुन्दं च जहौ सङ्कथया शुचः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: pṛthā bhrātṝn svasṝrvīkṣya tatputrān pitarāvapi bhrātṛpat‍nīrmukundaṃ ca jahau saṅkathayā śucaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: When Kuntī saw her brothers, sisters, their sons, her parents, sisters-in-law and Śrī Kṛṣṇa, she felt relief as her grief soothed during her conversation with them.

devanagari: कुन्त्युवाच । आर्य भ्रातरहं मन्ये आत्मानमकृताशिषम् । यद्वा आपत्सु मद्वार्तां नानुस्मरथ सत्तमाः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: kuntyuvāca ārya bhrātarahaṃ manye ātmānamakṛtāśiṣam yadvā āpatsu madvārtāṃ nānusmaratha sattamāḥ

translation:
Kunti said:
Dear (elder) brother! I feel I am unlucky as no desire of mine becomes fulfilled. Or it may be that excellent-most brothers like you do not remember of me, even though I am entangled in calamities.

devanagari: सुहृदो ज्ञातयः पुत्रा भ्रातरः पितरावपि । नानुस्मरन्ति स्वजनं यस्य दैवमदक्षिणम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: suhṛdo jñātayaḥ putrā bhrātaraḥ pitarāvapi nānusmaranti svajanaṃ yasya daivamadakṣiṇam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: It appears that friends, relatives, sons, brothers—nay even parents—do not remember their own relative if the Fate frowns upon him.

devanagari: श्रीवसुदेव उवाच । अम्ब मास्मानसूयेथा दैवक्रीडनकान् नरान् । ईशस्य हि वशे लोकः कुरुते कार्यतेऽथ वा ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīvasudeva uvāca amba māsmānasūyethā daivakrīḍanakān narān īśasya hi vaśe lokaḥ kurute kāryate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Vasudeva replied:
Mother (a term of endearment for younger sister)! Please do not blame us, who are ordinary mortals—toys or pawns in the hands of Providence. The world is completely under the control of the Almighty Lord and everyone acts or is compelled to act according to his will.

devanagari: कंसप्रतापिताः सर्वे वयं याता दिशं दिशम् । एतर्ह्येव पुनः स्थानं दैवेनासादिताः स्वसः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: kaṃsapratāpitāḥ sarve vayaṃ yātā diśaṃ diśam etarhyeva punaḥ sthānaṃ daivenāsāditāḥ svasaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ yā class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ yā class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ yā class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ yā class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ yā class 2 verb]

translation: (As you know it) due to the harassment of Karñsa all of us had to flee and were scattered in all directions. Dear sister! It is only recently that the Providence has rehabilitated us together.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । वसुदेवोग्रसेनाद्यैः यदुभिस्तेऽर्चिता नृपाः । आसन् अच्युतसन्दर्श परमानन्दनिर्वृताः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca vasudevograsenādyaiḥ yadubhiste āsan acyutasandarśa paramānandanirvṛtāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative plural from √ ṛc ], [vocative plural from √ ṛc ]
[nominative plural from √ ṛc ], [vocative plural from √ ṛc ], [accusative plural from √ ṛc ]
[nominative plural from √ ṛc ], [vocative plural from √ ṛc ]
[nominative plural from √ ṛc ], [vocative plural from √ ṛc ], [accusative plural from √ ṛc ]

translation:
Śrī Suka said:
The kings who were duly worshipped by Vasudeva, Ugrasena and other prominent Yādavas felt deeply satisfied and delighted at the sight of Lord Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: भीष्मो द्रोणोऽम्बिकापुत्रो गान्धारी ससुता तथा । सदाराः पाण्डवाः कुन्ती सञ्जयो विदुरः कृपः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: bhīṣmo droṇo sadārāḥ pāṇḍavāḥ kuntī sañjayo viduraḥ kṛpaḥ
translation: (Those kings arid prominent persons were) Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, Gāndhārī and all her sons, Pāṇḍavas and their queens, Kuntī, Sṛñjaya, Vidura, Kṛpa, Kuntibhoja. Virāṭa, Bhīṣmaka and Nagnajit, the mighty; Purujit, Drupada, Śalya, Dhṛṣṭaketu along with the king of Kāśī; Damaghoṣa, Viśālākṣa, the kings of Mithilā, Madra and Kekaya; Yudhāmanyu, Suśarmā, Bāhlīka along with his princes.

devanagari: कुन्तीभोजो विराटश्च भीष्मको नग्नजिन्महान् । पुरुजिद् द्रुपदः शल्यो धृष्टकेतुः सकाशिराट् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: kuntībhojo virāṭaśca bhīṣmako nagnajinmahān purujid drupadaḥ śalyo dhṛṣṭaketuḥ sakāśirāṭ
translation: (Those kings arid prominent persons were) Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, Gāndhārī and all her sons, Pāṇḍavas and their queens, Kuntī, Sṛñjaya, Vidura, Kṛpa, Kuntibhoja. Virāṭa, Bhīṣmaka and Nagnajit, the mighty; Purujit, Drupada, Śalya, Dhṛṣṭaketu along with the king of Kāśī; Damaghoṣa, Viśālākṣa, the kings of Mithilā, Madra and Kekaya; Yudhāmanyu, Suśarmā, Bāhlīka along with his princes.

devanagari: दमघोषो विशालाक्षो मैथिलो मद्रकेकयौ । युधामन्युः सुशर्मा च ससुता बाह्लिकादयः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: damaghoṣo viśālākṣo maithilo madrakekayau yudhāmanyuḥ suśarmā ca sasutā bāhlikādayaḥ
translation: (Those kings arid prominent persons were) Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the son of Ambikā, Gāndhārī and all her sons, Pāṇḍavas and their queens, Kuntī, Sṛñjaya, Vidura, Kṛpa, Kuntibhoja. Virāṭa, Bhīṣmaka and Nagnajit, the mighty; Purujit, Drupada, Śalya, Dhṛṣṭaketu along with the king of Kāśī; Damaghoṣa, Viśālākṣa, the kings of Mithilā, Madra and Kekaya; Yudhāmanyu, Suśarmā, Bāhlīka along with his princes.

devanagari: राजानो ये च राजेन्द्र युधिष्ठिरमनुव्रताः । श्रीनिकेतं वपुः शौरेः सस्त्रीकं वीक्ष्य विस्मिताः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: rājāno ye ca rājendra yudhiṣṭhiramanuvratāḥ śrīniketaṃ vapuḥ śaureḥ sastrīkaṃ vīkṣya vismitāḥ
translation: O king of kings! These princes and those who were the allies of Yudhiṣṭhira were astonished to behold the personality of Lord Kṛṣṇa which was the abode of the goddess Śrī (of affluence and beauty) in the company of his queens.

devanagari: अथ ते रामकृष्णाभ्यां सम्यक् प्राप्तसमर्हणाः । प्रशशंसुर्मुदा युक्ता वृष्णीन् कृष्णपरिग्रहान् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: atha te rāmakṛṣṇābhyāṃ samyak prāptasamarhaṇāḥ praśaśaṃsurmudā yuktā vṛṣṇīn kṛṣṇaparigrahān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb], [vocative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb]
[nominative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb], [vocative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb], [accusative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation: Thereupon, the kings who were duly honoured by Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa were greatly delighted and eloquently praised Vṛṣṇis who were the relatives of Lord Kṛṣṇa and were under his protection.

devanagari: अहो भोजपते यूयं जन्मभाजो नृणामिह । यत् पश्यथासकृत् कृष्णं दुर्दर्शमपि योगिनाम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: aho bhojapate yūyaṃ janmabhājo nṛṇāmiha yat paśyathāsakṛt kṛṣṇaṃ durdarśamapi yoginām

translation: (They complimented:) “O Ugrasena, the king of Bhojas! Inasmuch as you are lucky to have constantly the sight of lord Kṛṣṇa who is very difficult to be seen even by Yogins, you must deem to have accomplished the purpose of human existence among all men born in this world.

devanagari: यद्विश्रुतिः श्रुतिनुतेदमलं पुनाति । पादावनेजनपयश्च वचश्च शास्त्रम् । भूः कालभर्जितभगापि यदङ्घ्रिपद्म । स्पर्शोत्थशक्तिरभिवर्षति नोऽखि...
english_devnagari: yadviśrutiḥ śrutinutedamalaṃ punāti pādāvanejanapayaśca vacaśca śāstram bhūḥ kālabharjitabhagāpi yadaṅghripadma sparśotthaśaktirabhivarṣati no

[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: His spotless glory sung by the Vedas purifies the world; so does the water that washes his feet and his word, viz. the Vedic Śāstra[1]. The earth which has all its potential productive capacity burnt up by the Time-Spirit, has all her powers resuscitated by the touch of his lotus-feet. Hence, she yields to us all our desired objects.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Padaratnāvalī: The Śāstra such as The Brahmasūtras issued out of Lord Kṛṣṇa’s mouth purifies the world.

devanagari: तद्दर्शनस्पर्शनानुपथप्रजल्प । शय्यासनाशनसयौनसपिण्डबन्धः । येषां गृहे निरयवर्त्मनि वर्ततां वः । स्वर्गापवर्गविरमः स्वयमास विष्णुः ॥ ३१ ...
english_devnagari: taddarśanasparśanānupathaprajalpa śayyāsanāśanasayaunasapiṇḍabandhaḥ yeṣāṃ gṛhe nirayavartmani vartatāṃ vaḥ svargāpavargaviramaḥ svayamāsa viṣṇuḥ

[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: The householder’s life paves the way to hell. But though you lead that (householder’s) life, Lord Viṣṇu. himself who makes one unattached both to celestial life as well as salvation (mokṣa) stays in your houses, and you can see him, touch, him, go after him, speak with him, sit close to him, eat with him, share the bed with him and have contacts and relations with him.[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Padaratnāvalī: You have really accomplished the highest purpose in your life as you are in direct contact with Lord Viṣṇu—The source of the bliss in the heaven and in the Mokṣa. He of his own accord has been in your house, so in your case householder’s life will not lead to hell.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । नन्दस्तत्र यदून् प्राप्तान् ज्ञात्वा कृष्णपुरोगमान् । तत्रागमद्वृतो गोपैः अनः स्थार्थैः दिदृक्षया ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca nandastatra yadūn prāptān jñātvā kṛṣṇapurogamān tatrāgamadvṛto gopaiḥ anaḥ sthārthaiḥ didṛkṣayā

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Learning that Yādavas of whom Kṛṣṇa is prominent, have arrived there, Nanda accompanied with Gopas and carts loaded with milk-products, came there with a desire to see them.

devanagari: तं दृष्ट्वा वृष्णयो हृष्टाः तन्वः प्राणमिवोत्थिताः । परिषस्वजिरे गाढं चिरदर्शनकातराः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ dṛṣṭvā vṛṣṇayo hṛṣṭāḥ tanvaḥ prāṇamivotthitāḥ pariṣasvajire gāḍhaṃ ciradarśanakātarāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ hṛṣ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛṣ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb]

translation: Seeing Nanda all the Yādavas were overjoyed and rose up to receive him, as the bodies do after the return of vital breath. Being extremely anxious to see him for a long time, they embraced him closely.

devanagari: वसुदेवः परिष्वज्य सम्प्रीतः प्रेमविह्वलः । स्मरन् कंसकृतान् क्लेशान् पुत्रन्यासं च गोकुले ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: vasudevaḥ pariṣvajya samprītaḥ premavihvalaḥ smaran kaṃsakṛtān kleśān putranyāsaṃ ca gokule

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]
[accusative plural from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Vasudeva was especially pleased. Being overwhelmed with affection, he embraced him fast, remembering how Kaṃsa subjected him to harrowing persecutions and how he had to entrust his son to Nanda’s care at his Gokula.

devanagari: कृष्णरामौ परिष्वज्य पितरावभिवाद्य च । न किञ्चनोचतुः प्रेम्णा साश्रुकण्ठौ कुरूद्वह ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇarāmau pariṣvajya pitarāvabhivādya ca na kiñcanocatuḥ premṇā sāśrukaṇṭhau kurūdvaha
translation: Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma embraced and bowed to their (foster) parents. But due to overwhelming affection, and the throat being choked with emotions (lit. tears of joy) they could not utter a word, O best of Kurus.

devanagari: तावात्मासनमारोप्य बाहुभ्यां परिरभ्य च । यशोदा च महाभागा सुतौ विजहतुः शुचः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: tāvātmāsanamāropya bāhubhyāṃ parirabhya ca yaśodā ca mahābhāgā sutau vijahatuḥ śucaḥ

[nominative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: The highly blessed Yaśodā placed both the sons on her seat (v.l. on her lap) and clasping them together in her arms in close embrace, forgot her (long-felt) distress (of separation from them).

devanagari: रोहिणी देवकी चाथ परिष्वज्य व्रजेश्वरीम् । स्मरन्त्यौ तत्कृतां मैत्रीं बाष्पकण्ठ्यौ समूचतुः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: rohiṇī devakī cātha pariṣvajya vrajeśvarīm smarantyau tatkṛtāṃ maitrīṃ bāṣpakaṇṭhyau samūcatuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ smṛ class 1 verb]
[accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: Remembering their close friendship with Yaśodā, both Rohiṇī and Devakī embraced her. With their voice choked with emotions (tears), they exclaimed in faltering tones.

devanagari: का विस्मरेत वां मैत्रीं अनिवृत्तां व्रजेश्वरि । अवाप्याप्यैन्द्रमैश्वर्यं यस्या नेह प्रतिक्रिया ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: kā vismareta vāṃ maitrīṃ anivṛttāṃ vrajeśvari avāpyāpyaindramaiśvaryaṃ yasyā neha pratikriyā
translation: “O queen of Vraja! Who can ever forget your everlasting (act of) friendship. Even if one attains to the fortune and status of the Lord of Heaven, the great debt (of your friendship) is unrepayable in this world.

devanagari: एतावदृष्टपितरौ युवयोः स्म पित्रोः । सम्प्रीणनाभ्युदयपोषणपालनानि । प्राप्योषतुर्भवति पक्ष्म ह यद्वदक्ष्णोः । न्यस्तावकुत्र च भयौ न सतां...
english_devnagari: etāvadṛṣṭapitarau yuvayoḥ sma pitroḥ samprīṇanābhyudayapoṣaṇapālanāni prāpyoṣaturbhavati pakṣma ha yadvadakṣṇoḥ nyastāvakutra ca bhayau na satāṃ paraḥ svaḥ
translation: Worshipful lady! These two children who had not seen their own parents, were entrusted to your filial care. They lived under your care like eyes protected by eye-lids without any fear from any quarter. You gave them protection, nourishment, loving care, caressing growth and development. For the good and righteous persons do not discriminate between a child who is theirs and the one who is not theirs.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । गोप्यश्च कृष्णमुपलभ्य चिरादभीष्टं । यत्प्रेक्षणे दृशिषु पक्ष्मकृतं शपन्ति । दृग्भिर्हृदीकृतमलं परिरभ्य सर्वाः । तद्भावम...
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca gopyaśca kṛṣṇamupalabhya cirādabhīṣṭaṃ yatprekṣaṇe dṛśiṣu pakṣmakṛtaṃ śapanti dṛgbhirhṛdīkṛtamalaṃ parirabhya sarvāḥ tadbhāvamāpurapi nityayujāṃ durāpam

[nominative plural from √ śap class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ śap class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ śap class 1 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having seen their beloved Kṛṣṇa after a pretty long time, they (yearned to gaze at him continuously without the interruption of winking of the eyelids and so) they blamed the Creator who created the eyelids and thus interrupted (even for a wink), his vision. Mentally they closely embraced him, who was ushered in their hearts through their eyes, and instantly became merged in him—an accomplishment not within the easy reach of Yogins who always practice to attain one-ness with him through Yogic meditation.

devanagari: भगवांस्तास्तथाभूता विविक्त उपसङ्गतः । आश्लिष्यानामयं पृष्ट्वा प्रहसन् इदमब्रवीत् ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavāṃstāstathābhūtā vivikta upasaṅgataḥ āśliṣyānāmayaṃ pṛṣṭvā prahasan idamabravīt
translation: (Kṛṣṇa assumed as many forms as there were Gopīs. And at night in a secluded place simultaneously met them all severally). When the Lord saw them in that (absorbed) state of meditation, he met each of them, in privacy, embraced them, enquired about their health and spoke to them smilingly.

devanagari: अपि स्मरथ नः सख्यः स्वानामर्थचिकीर्षया । गतांश्चिरायिताञ्छत्रु पक्षक्षपणचेतसः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: api smaratha naḥ sakhyaḥ svānāmarthacikīrṣayā gatāṃścirāyitāñchatru pakṣakṣapaṇacetasaḥ
translation: “O friends! Do you remember us who in order to serve the cause of our relatives have left Vraja and who were busy, as our heart was distracted elsewhere in annihilating the party of our enemies, and hence, were out of sight and have returned after a long time.

devanagari: अप्यवध्यायथास्मान् स्विदकृतज्ञाविशङ्कया । नूनं भूतानि भगवान् युनक्ति वियुनक्ति च ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: apyavadhyāyathāsmān svidakṛtajñāviśaṅkayā nūnaṃ bhūtāni bhagavān yunakti viyunakti ca
translation: As a matter of fact, it is the will of the Almighty Lord which unites or disunites beings. Do you form a low opinion about us suspecting us to be ungrateful to you (despite your consistent friendliness during our stay at Gokul [Gokula?])? (Our absence was an act of God. Please do not misunderstand us).

devanagari: वायुर्यथा घनानीकं तृणं तूलं रजांसि च । संयोज्याक्षिपते भूयः तथा भूतानि भूतकृत् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: vāyuryathā ghanānīkaṃ tṛṇaṃ tūlaṃ rajāṃsi ca saṃyojyākṣipate bhūyaḥ tathā bhūtāni bhūtakṛt
translation: Like unto the wind bringing together, or dispersing clouds, blades of grass, cotton, particles of dust, the Lord—the creator unites or disunites beings created by him.

devanagari: मयि भक्तिर्हि भूतानां अमृतत्वाय कल्पते । दिष्ट्या यदासीत् मत्स्नेहो भवतीनां मदापनः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: mayi bhaktirhi bhūtānāṃ amṛtatvāya kalpate diṣṭyā yadāsīt matsneho bhavatīnāṃ madāpanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: It is a matter of great joy and fortune that the loving devotion entertained by you for me shall lead to my Self-realization and absorption in me, for it is only loving (and constant) devotion unto me that leads people to immortality i.e. Final Liberation.

devanagari: अहं हि सर्वभूतानां आदिरन्तोऽन्तरं बहिः । भौतिकानां यथा खं वार्भूर्वायुर्ज्योतिरङ्गनाः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ hi sarvabhūtānāṃ ādiranto bhautikānāṃ yathā khaṃ vārbhūrvāyurjyotiraṅganāḥ
translation: O beautiful damsels! Just as the elements such as the sky, water, earth, wind and fire constitute the inside and outside, beginning and the end of all material objects, I am really the beginning and the end of all beings permeating them all from within and without.

devanagari: एवं ह्येतानि भूतानि भूतेष्वात्माऽत्मना ततः । उभयं मय्यथ परे पश्यताभातमक्षरे ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ hyetāni bhūtāni bhūteṣvātmā ubhayaṃ mayyatha pare paśyatābhātamakṣare
translation: In this way all these elements constitute all (gross) products and beings (and are thus in them but they are not in the Soul). The Soul abides in them as the enjoyer and thus pervades them. You, therefore, observe clearly that both these (The soul and the elements) appear in me, but I as the Supreme Perfect Brahman transcend them, all.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । अध्यात्मशिक्षया गोप्य एवं कृष्णेन शिक्षिताः । तदनुस्मरणध्वस्त जीवकोशास्तमध्यगन् ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca adhyātmaśikṣayā gopya evaṃ kṛṣṇena śikṣitāḥ tadanusmaraṇadhvasta jīvakośāstamadhyagan

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
In this way, the Gopīs were initiated and instructed in the spiritual Lore by Lord Kṛṣṇa. By dint of constant contemplation on him, they destroyed the sheath of the subtle body (jīva-kośa) and became merged in him.

devanagari: आहुश्च ते नलिननाभ पदारविन्दं । योगेश्वरैर्हृदि विचिन्त्यमगाधबोधैः । संसारकूपपतितोत्तरणावलम्बं । गेहं जुषामपि मनस्युदियात् सदा नः ॥ ४९ ...
english_devnagari: āhuśca te nalinanābha padāravindaṃ yogeśvarairhṛdi vicintyamagādhabodhaiḥ saṃsārakūpapatitottaraṇāvalambaṃ gehaṃ juṣāmapi manasyudiyāt sadā naḥ
translation:
And they prayed: “O Lotus-navelled Lord! May your lotus-feet worthy of being meditated upon in their hearts by masters of Yoga of unfathomable spiritual wisdom and which form as a support (or supporting plank) to get out of the well of saṃsāra to people who have fallen into that well, may these lotus feet ever remain manifest in our hearts even though we lead a domestic life in Saṃsāra.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । वृष्णीगोपसंगमो नाम द्व्यशीतितमोऽध्यायः ॥ ८२ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhe vṛṣṇīgopasaṃgamo nāma dvyaśītitamo
